notes: 5 repeated searches after 2 warmup turns; benchmark from scripts/run-benchmarks.sh profile
Added independent TT verification key and collision regression test; no measured node change on profile baseline
Principal variation search reduced repeated-search throughput versus baseline; reverted
TT move-to-front ordering reduced repeated-search throughput versus baseline; reverted
Direct-index TT table with simple overwrite policy did not improve repeated-search node count; reverted
Iterative-deepening root preferred move ordering reduced repeated-search throughput versus baseline; reverted
Incremental leaf evaluation preserved behavior but did not improve repeated-search node count; reverted
Generic killer-move ordering reduced repeated-search throughput versus baseline; reverted
Added static move-ordering bonuses for word length, edge progress, and bomb contact; repeated-search baseline improved and reproduced
Extra near-edge bonus reduced repeated-search throughput versus v1; reverted
Removing bomb and megabomb bonuses reduced repeated-search throughput versus v1; reverted
Endpoint-row bonus reduced repeated-search throughput versus v1; reverted
Increasing the length bonus above v1 reduced repeated-search throughput; reverted
Increasing the progress bonus above v1 reduced repeated-search throughput; reverted
Lowering the progress bonus was close to v1 but still slightly worse; reverted
Lowering bomb and megabomb bonuses reduced repeated-search throughput versus v1; reverted
Forward-span bonus reduced repeated-search throughput versus v1; reverted
Skipping already-owned cells in recordOne preserved behavior but did not improve the repeated-search baseline
Iterative bomb propagation in recordOne preserved behavior but did not improve the repeated-search baseline; reverted
State-aware front-window reranking by claimable and enemy path cells reduced repeated-search throughput badly versus v1; reverted
Adding a weak future-mobility bonus from per-square legal-word counts underperformed the stronger variants
A moderate future-mobility bonus improved profile slightly but was dominated by stronger scaling
A stronger future-mobility bonus improved profile but still underperformed divisor1
Added a per-square future-mobility bonus from legal-word counts across the claimed path; profile rose from 275945 to 339260 and short rose from 81916 to 84370
Shrinking the move cap aggressively by ply cut away too much search and reduced both short and repeated-search benchmarks
Extra forward-weighted mobility on top of raw mobility improved short slightly but lost on repeated search
A weak future-move diversity bonus was worse than the stronger diversity variants
A moderate future-move diversity bonus improved on raw mobility but was beaten by divisor2
Overweighting future-move diversity collapsed the search and dropped completed depth
Added a future-move diversity bonus based on unique legal starts unlocked by the claimed path; profile rose from 339260 to 354475 and short rose to 87822
TT hash-move ordering still reduced throughput badly even after the stronger Wordbase ordering baseline; reverted
Biasing diversity toward the deepest claimed cells looked promising in one run but was not repeatable and was reverted
Using the best future word from claimed anchors overfit flashy lines and collapsed repeated-search depth
Counting long future words per square was another unstable anchor-quality signal and was reverted
Added a long-word diversity bonus so paths preserving many distinct long threats are ordered earlier; profile rose from 354475 to 364230
Use_find_first_no_behavior_change
Added a small bend penalty to prefer straighter lanes; repeated-search nodes regressed slightly vs baseline 364230 so reverting
No measurable change versus baseline; reverted to divisor8

Added a rare-letter tie-breaker favoring paths that claim scarce letters (e.g., the lone 'z' along GLAMORIZE). On the profile scenario total_nodes rose from 364230 to 364270 and deepest_completed_depth stayed 3; effect is tiny but repeatable.
Added a very weak 8-neighbor halo bonus; repeated-search depth collapsed from 3 to 2, so reverting
Added a max-halo neighborhood tie-breaker (3x3 local mobility
column10: single best cell per path) with a stiff divisor; profile repeated-search nodes unchanged vs baseline so reverting

notes: After implementing the rare-letter tie-breaker, repeated-search totals matched baseline (no throughput or depth change). Keeping tree clean and reverting.
Added a row-band diversity bonus that rewards paths spanning many distinct rows (e.g., GLAMORIZE diagonal vs GLASS horizontal). Repeated-search totals unchanged vs baseline; reverting.
Added a small column-band diversity bonus so diagonals like GLAMORIZE beat one-file paths like EEL when other signals tie. Repeated-search totals reproduced exactly at baseline, so reverting.

Added a frontier mobility bonus from unique neighboring squares around the claimed path (e.g., GLAMORIZE leaves side shoulders; EEL mostly stacks starts on one file). At divisor 6 the repeated-search profile stayed exactly at baseline, so trying a stronger scaling.

Strengthening the frontier neighboring-word-count bonus reordered search in the wrong direction: repeated-search profile fell from 364230 to 345895 while depth stayed 3. Reverting that scaling and trying a less correlated frontier signal.

Switched to a geometry-only frontier count that rewards paths exposing many neighboring squares. It was even noisier: repeated-search profile fell to 340310 with no extra depth, so reverting to the known-good baseline.

Counted distinct future word texts so a lane leaving {stare, stern, stone} would outrank one mostly preserving repeated GLAMORIZE placements. At divisor 4 the repeated-search profile matched the 364230 baseline exactly, so reverting.

Strengthening the distinct future word-text bonus still produced the exact 364230 repeated-search profile total with depth 3. The text-level signal was too correlated with the existing mobility/diversity stack, so reverting.
Rewarded paths whose future follow-ups land on several forward rows instead of one band. That reordered the profile position badly enough to stall at depth 2 on every repeated search, so reverting.
Rewarded paths that preserved many distinct future start squares. That favored noisy multi-anchor clutter over the baseline ordering and every repeated search stalled at depth 2, so reverting.
Restricted the start-square coverage bonus to long follow-ups only, hoping to favor separate long-threat anchors. It still shoved the profile search into the same bad depth-2 ordering, so reverting.

Summed per-square counts of long words along the claimed path so the middle of a GLAMORIZE lane would outrank side squares that mostly feed GLASS. The signal was far too strong: repeated-search depth fell from 3 to 2, so reverting.

Added a small per-square forward-reach bonus based on the average depth of words through each claimed cell, so lanes used by deeper attacks like PERILLED break ties over squares whose words stall locally. The repeated-search profile improved reproducibly from 364230 to 364570 in two runs, and short held at 87981.

Changed the retained square forward-reach signal to count only rows still ahead of each claimed square, hoping middle cells on lanes like PERILLED or GLAMORIZE would beat late squares that are already deep. That stripped too much correlation with raw progress: repeated-search profile collapsed from 364570 to 17775 and stalled at depth 2, so reverting.

Gated rare-letter credit so only squares above the board's average forward reach contributed, aiming to keep the GLAMORIZE-style Z bonus while muting shallow side-branch junk. Repeated-search profile slipped from the retained 364570 baseline to 364390 at the same depth 3, so reverting without a suite run.

Scaled each rare-letter tie-breaker by how far the claimed square's forward reach sat above the board average, so deep-lane rares like the GLAMORIZE z kept some credit while shallow side branches lost it. Repeated-search profile reproduced the exact 364570 baseline in all five runs, so reverting as flat.

Counted claimed cells whose forward-reach score cleared the board average plus one row, then gave a tiny bonus only when a path had several such cells so uniform lanes like PERILLED could beat mixed paths with one strong GLAMORIZE anchor. Repeated-search profile matched the 364570 baseline in all five runs, so reverting as flat.

Summed only the forward-reach excess above the board-wide average for each claimed cell, hoping lanes with several strong interior squares like PERILLED would beat paths carried by one GLAMORIZE-style anchor. Repeated-search profile dropped reproducibly from 364570 to 364200 in all five runs at depth 3, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a gated consistency bonus for paths with at least two cells at or above the board-average forward-reach score, hoping uniformly strong lanes like PERILLED would beat a GLAMORIZE anchor padded by shallow cleanup cells. The repeated-search profile became unstable immediately: one measured run dropped to depth 2 and the total fell well below the 364570 baseline, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a one-point tie-breaker when a path had at least three cells at or above the board-average forward-reach score, aiming to reward uniform lanes like PERILLED over lines carried by one GLAMORIZE anchor. Repeated-search profile slipped from the 364570 baseline to 364350 at depth 3, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a one-point tie-breaker when at least two claimed cells cleared board-average forward reach plus one row, trying to reward genuinely strong interior lanes like PERILLED without crediting a single GLAMORIZE anchor. The repeated-search profile became unstable again: one measured run fell to depth 2 and the total collapsed far below the 364570 baseline, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a one-point tie-breaker when at least four claimed cells met the board-average forward-reach score, aiming to reward uniformly deep lanes like PERILLED over paths carried by a single GLAMORIZE anchor. The repeated-search profile became unstable immediately: one measured run fell to depth 2 and total_nodes collapsed well below the 364570 baseline, so reverting without a suite run.

Gated each square-word-count contribution so cells with forward reach under two rows only kept half credit, aiming to preserve GLAMORIZE/PERILLED lanes while damping EEL-style cleanup pockets. The first profile run looked strong at 367935 total nodes, but the immediate rerun became unstable with four repeated-search turns falling to depth 2 and total_nodes collapsing to 87711, so reverting without a suite run.

Discounted each square-word-count contribution by a smooth function of that cell's side-to-move forward reach, so reach-0 cleanup pockets like EEL kept half credit while deeper GLAMORIZE/PERILLED cells kept more. The repeated-search profile was a clear depth-3 regression from the 364570 baseline, so reverting without a suite run.

Only halved square-word-count credit on cells whose side-to-move forward reach was exactly zero, intending to hit dead-end EEL-style cleanup pockets without touching reach-1+ GLAMORIZE/PERILLED lanes. The repeated-search profile became unstable anyway, with two measured turns dropping to depth 2, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a small goalward-only next-step branching bonus from each claimed start square, counting distinct non-retreating continuations so PERILLED/INTERROBANG-style lanes could beat sideways cleanup starts like EEL. The repeated-search profile was stable but slipped from the 364570 baseline to 361790 total nodes, so reverting without a suite run.

Counted only excess goalward next-step fanout per claimed square, so ordinary single-lane continuations stayed neutral while PERILLED/INTERROBANG-style branch points still broke ties over EEL-style cleanup pockets. The repeated-search profile improved reproducibly from 364570 to 365465 at depth 3, and profile-suite also improved overall: readme-main 364570->365465, driver-sample-a 298205->304875, driver-sample-b 2010->1960.

Tried doubling the weight of the kept excess goalward next-step fanout signal so PERILLED/INTERROBANG-style branch points would break ties more aggressively over EEL-style cleanup pockets. The repeated-search profile stayed stable at depth 3 but slipped from the 365465 baseline to 364880 total nodes, so reverting without a suite run.

Tightened the kept excess goalward next-step fanout signal so only row-advancing continuations counted, aiming to keep PERILLED-style forward forks while dropping same-row EEL cleanups. The repeated-search profile stayed stable at depth 3 but slipped from the 365465 baseline to 364730 total nodes, so reverting without a suite run.

Required each counted goalward next-step branch to land on a square whose own average forward reach still extended beyond that landing row, aiming to keep PERILLED-style deeper forks while filtering EEL-style cleanup branches that immediately peter out. The repeated-search profile stayed stable at depth 3 but regressed from the 365465 baseline to 363945 total nodes, so reverting without a suite run.

Prototype cached policy-overlay move ordering with disk-loaded default weights over broad static signals such as progress, forward reach, goalward branching, and long-fork diversity. The repeated-search profile collapsed immediately from the 365465 baseline to 22955 total nodes and every measured turn fell to depth 2, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a small per-square long-word count bonus so GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style lanes could beat EEL-style cleanup pockets even when both touched similar raw word counts. The repeated-search profile improved reproducibly from 365465 to 365485 at depth 3, but the gain was only +20 total nodes across two runs, so reverting as too marginal to keep without a suite run.

Added a small per-square bonus for distinct long word texts, so lanes supporting several different long attacks like GLAMORIZE/GLAMORIZED/GLAMORIZER could beat cleanup pockets with many overlapping long paths. The repeated-search profile stayed at depth 3 but regressed clearly from the 365465 baseline to 360390 total nodes, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a small per-square bonus for distinct goal depths reached by words through each claimed cell, aiming to reward PERILLED-style lanes that can finish on several rows over EEL-style pockets that mostly recycle one shallow finish row. The repeated-search profile collapsed immediately from the 365465 baseline to 17770 total nodes and every measured turn fell to depth 2, so reverting without a suite run.

Added an optional disk-loaded policy overlay that attached a per-move float score and gave a tiny averaged lane-quality bonus, aiming to let focused lanes like PERILLED outrank shallow cleanup words like EEL without swamping the base heuristic. The repeated-search profile improved reproducibly from the 365465 baseline to 365525 at depth 3, but the gain was only +60 total nodes across two runs, so reverting as too marginal to keep without a suite run.

Added a small per-square bonus for average remaining goalward reach beyond each claimed cell, so middle launch squares with several rows of runway left like PERILLED could outrank already-advanced cleanup pockets like EEL that usually finish immediately. The repeated-search profile improved reproducibly from the 365465 baseline to 365605 at depth 3, and profile-suite also improved overall: readme-main 365465->365605, driver-sample-a 304875->304925, driver-sample-b held at 1960 while staying depth 4.

Added an optional WORDBASE_POLICY_TABLE hook that loaded broad board-derived overlay weights from disk and used the resulting float as a secondary sort key only, aiming to break PERILLED-style lane ties over EEL-style cleanup pockets without changing evaluation. With forward-reach, remaining-runway, branching, and diversity weights enabled, the repeated-search profile regressed clearly from the 365605 baseline to 364935 total nodes at depth 3, so reverting without a suite run.

Retried the optional disk-loaded policy overlay as an even narrower tie-break using only average remaining goalward reach and excess goalward branching, hoping to reproduce the earlier marginal overlay win while staying aligned with the strongest static lane signals. The repeated-search profile landed at the same regressed 364935 total nodes versus the 365605 baseline while staying at depth 3, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a small per-square bonus for average remaining goalward letters after each claimed cell, aiming to favor PERILLED-style interior lanes with several letters of follow-through over EEL-style cleanup pockets that usually end immediately. The repeated-search profile collapsed immediately from the 365605 baseline to 17775 total nodes and every measured turn fell to depth 2, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a small per-square bonus for the average remaining goalward reach of each immediate goalward continuation, aiming to favor PERILLED-style lanes whose next steps still have runway over EEL-style cleanup pockets whose next cells are nearly finished. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from the 365605 baseline to 364935 total nodes at depth 3, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a small per-square bonus for how many long words cross each claimed cell, aiming to favor PERILLED-style launch lanes that sit inside several deep attacks over EEL-style cleanup pockets whose traffic is mostly short finishers. The repeated-search profile collapsed immediately from the 365605 baseline to 17775 total nodes and every measured turn fell to depth 2, so reverting without a suite run.

Added an optional disk-loaded linear policy overlay that assigned each move a float score from broad board-derived features and used it only to refine equal-goodness ordering, aiming to give PERILLED-style launch lanes a smoother tie-break over EEL-style cleanup pockets without changing evaluation. The repeated-search profile reproduced at 365640 total nodes and depth 3 in two runs versus the 365605 baseline, but the +35-node gain was too marginal to treat as a clear win, so reverting without a suite run.

Added an optional disk-loaded policy table stub that attached a per-move float score from broad static lane signals and weighted the opening square's remaining runway and goalward fanout to break equal-goodness ties, aiming to let PERILLED-style launch anchors outrank EEL-style cleanup pockets without changing evaluation. With the default overlay enabled, the repeated-search profile regressed clearly from the 365605 baseline to 364915 total nodes at depth 3, so reverting without a suite run.

Added a composite per-square bonus that multiplied excess goalward branching by remaining goalward runway, aiming to reward PERILLED-style interior forks that can still branch for several rows over EEL-style cleanup forks near the finish. At divisor 8 the repeated-search profile collapsed immediately from the 365605 baseline to 17775 total nodes and every measured turn fell to depth 2, so reverting and retrying only with a much weaker scale.

Retried the same branch-times-runway signal as a much smaller tie-break so only interior PERILLED-style forks with real runway could separate from EEL-style cleanup forks. The repeated-search profile reproduced the 365605 baseline exactly at depth 3, so the feature was effectively flat and I reverted without a suite run.

Added TT-first killer/history move ordering plus conservative late-move reductions in `gtsa.hpp`, aiming to let repeated cutoffs promote the same broad launch lanes while only trimming late non-TT cleanup tries. The repeated-search profile regressed catastrophically from the 365605 baseline to 8345 total nodes at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Retried the search-tuning assignment with only TT-first killer/history move ordering and no reductions, so repeated beta-cut launch lanes could float above later cleanup tries while preserving the existing board-derived fallback order. After forcing a rebuild of `perf-test`, the repeated-search profile still regressed clearly from the 365605 baseline to 167580 total nodes at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Tried the narrowest follow-up in `gtsa.hpp`: if a transposition-table move was still legal, rotate only that move to the front and otherwise preserve the existing board-derived static order, aiming to replay proven INTERROBANG/PERILLED lanes before later cleanup tries without introducing global killer or history promotion. The repeated-search profile reproduced the same 167580 total nodes and 33516 average nodes-per-turn regression at depth 3, so TT-first ordering alone was also a clear loss and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 16 words and then grow the excess at one quarter speed, aiming to keep GLAMORIZE-style rich spines ahead of dead cells while stopping overcrowded overlap pockets from outranking cleaner deep lanes like PERILLED. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from the 365605 baseline to 293210 total nodes and 58642 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 16 words and count any excess at half speed, aiming to preserve GLAMORIZE-style rich spines while stopping overlap-heavy hotspots from outranking cleaner PERILLED-style lanes on raw duplicate density alone. The repeated-search profile still regressed clearly from the 365605 baseline to 338240 total nodes and 67648 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Added a small per-square bonus for the average total row span of words through each claimed cell, aiming to let interior PERILLED-style squares inside long lanes beat crowded EEL-style cleanup pockets that mostly recycle nearby rows. The repeated-search profile improved reproducibly but only marginally from the 365605 baseline to 365765 total nodes and 73153 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so the gain was too small to treat as a clear win and I reverted without a suite run.

Retried the same average goalward-span signal at twice the strength so long multi-row lanes could separate more decisively from dense local clutter. The repeated-search profile still reproduced only a marginal gain versus baseline at 365690 total nodes and 73138 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, smaller than the divisor-8 variant, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 16 words and then count any excess at three-quarter speed, aiming to keep GLAMORIZE-style rich spines ahead of dead cells while trimming the leverage of extremely crowded overlap pockets versus cleaner PERILLED-style lanes. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from the 365605 baseline to 346660 total nodes and 69332 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 32 words and then count any excess at half speed, aiming to preserve genuinely rich GLAMORIZE/PERILLED launch lanes while trimming the leverage of extremely crowded overlap pockets. The repeated-search profile regressed clearly from the 365605 baseline to 333760 total nodes and 66752 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Clamped each claimed square's raw word-count contribution at 64 in src/board.h so ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes would stay unchanged while only the most overloaded overlap pockets lost leverage. The repeated-search profile regressed sharply from the 365605 baseline to 289910 total nodes and 57982 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 64 words and then count any excess at seven-eighths speed, aiming to leave ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes untouched while trimming only the most overcrowded duplicate-heavy pockets. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the 365605 baseline to 350875 total nodes and 70175 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 128 words and then count any excess at seven-eighths speed, aiming to touch only the most overloaded duplicate-heavy pockets while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes unchanged. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the 365605 baseline to 354005 total nodes and 70801 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to add a fixed extra premium of up to 16 words per claimed square, aiming to keep GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes ahead of dead cells while stopping 100-word overlap pockets from pulling far ahead on duplicate density alone. The repeated-search profile failed immediately by dropping from the 365605 baseline at depth 3 to 16845 total nodes and 3369 average nodes-per-turn at depth 2, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 128 words and then discount only one-sixteenth of any excess, aiming to leave ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes effectively unchanged while barely trimming the most overloaded overlap pockets. Even this very gentle tail reduction still regressed the repeated-search profile from the 365605 baseline to 354780 total nodes and 70956 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 256 words and then discount just one-thirty-second of any excess, aiming to touch only the most overloaded duplicate-heavy pockets while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style lanes unchanged. The repeated-search profile reproduced the exact 365605 baseline with 73121 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so the transform was effectively inert on the benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 192 words and then discount just one-thirty-second of any excess, aiming to leave ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes untouched while barely trimming only the busiest overlap hubs. The repeated-search profile again reproduced the exact 365605 baseline with 73121 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so this more conservative threshold was still effectively inert on the benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 160 words and then discount just one-thirty-second of any excess, aiming to leave ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style launch lanes intact while shaving only the far-tail leverage of the busiest duplicate-heavy overlap hubs. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the 365605 baseline to 360600 total nodes and 72120 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so even this narrow saturation hurt the benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 176 words and then discount just one-sixty-fourth of any excess, aiming to touch only the narrow band between the inert 192 cutoff and the regressing 160 cutoff while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style launch lanes effectively unchanged. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the 365605 baseline to 361465 total nodes and 72293 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so even this nearly inert tail trim hurt the benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

I first inspected the README benchmark board's raw mSquareWordCounts distribution and found only one square above 224 words and only two above 192, so I tried the narrowest non-inert cap I could justify: clamp each claimed square's contribution at 208 so only the single 231-word overlap hotspot lost weight while ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes stayed unchanged. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the 365605 baseline to 364570 total nodes and 72914 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, matching the older pre-goalward-reach baseline rather than the current best, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 224 words and then count any excess at half speed, aiming to touch only the single 231-word overlap hotspot while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style lanes unchanged. The repeated-search profile regressed from the 365605 baseline to 365465 total nodes and 73093 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, landing exactly on the older pre-remaining-goalward-reach baseline, so I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 224 words and then count any excess at three-quarters speed, aiming for the smallest non-inert trim to the single 231-word overlap hotspot while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style lanes unchanged. The repeated-search profile reproduced the exact 365605 baseline with 73121 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so this softer saturation was effectively inert on the benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 208 words and then count any excess at seven-eighths speed, aiming to leave ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style launch lanes untouched while trimming a slightly wider far-tail band than the inert 224-threshold trials. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the 365605 baseline to 365480 total nodes and 73096 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so even this very small tail discount hurt the benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 192 words and then count any excess at fifteen-sixteenths speed, so ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style launch lanes stayed exact while only the two hottest overlap hubs lost a couple of points. The repeated-search profile reproduced the exact 365605 baseline with 73121 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so this tail trim was effectively inert on the benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 96 words and then count any excess at three-quarters speed, aiming to compress crowded overlap hubs across the whole upper half of the board instead of only the 200-plus-word tail. That broad saturation badly overcorrected: the repeated-search profile fell from the 365605 baseline to 348830 total nodes and 69766 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so I reverted immediately without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 184 words and then count any excess at fifteen-sixteenths speed, aiming to touch a few more crowded overlap hubs than the inert 192-threshold trial while staying much gentler than the regressing broad discounts. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the 365605 baseline to 361485 total nodes and 72297 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so even this light extra taper hurt the README benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 128 words and then count any excess at fifteen-sixteenths speed, aiming to preserve ordinary GLAMORIZE-style rich lanes while lightly trimming medium-to-heavy overlap hubs whose duplicate local words might overstate their value. The repeated-search profile regressed sharply from the 365605 baseline to 354715 total nodes and 70943 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so even this smoother whole-board compression hurt the README benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 208 words and then count any excess at fifteen-sixteenths speed, aiming to touch only the single hottest overlap hub while leaving ordinary GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes exact. The repeated-search profile reproduced the exact 365605 baseline with 73121 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so this near-flat tail trim was inert on the benchmark board and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to hard-cap each claimed cell at 200 words, after checking the README-board histogram and seeing a gap above the top two overload hubs at 231 and 206 while GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes stayed at 189 and below. That still regressed the repeated-search profile from the 365605 baseline to 363730 total nodes with 72746 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so trimming even just those two hottest squares hurt move ordering and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 224 words and then count any excess at half speed, aiming to trim just the single 231-word overlap hotspot while leaving the 206-word GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style lanes exact. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the 365605 baseline to 365465 total nodes with 73093 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so even this isolated top-tail discount hurt move ordering and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 224 words and then count any excess at seven-eighths speed, so the README-board's single 231-word overlap hotspot lost just one point while the 206-word GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style rich lanes stayed exact. The repeated-search profile reproduced the exact 365605 baseline with 73121 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so this ultra-narrow tail trim was inert and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 224 words and then count any excess at three-quarters speed, so the README-board's single 231-word overlap hotspot lost two points while the 206-word GLAMORIZE/PERILLED-style lanes stayed exact. The repeated-search profile still reproduced the exact 365605 baseline with 73121 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so this midpoint trim between the regressing half-speed and inert seven-eighths variants was also flat and I reverted without a suite run.

Changed only squareWordCountBonus in src/board.h to keep each cell linear through 192 words and then count any excess at seven-eighths speed, so the two 200-plus overlap hubs lost a few points while the 189-word GLAMORIZE/PERILLED lanes stayed exact. The repeated-search profile still regressed from the 365605 baseline to 365430 total nodes with 73086 average nodes-per-turn at depth 3, so this middle-ground taper also hurt move ordering and I reverted without a suite run.
